items.briefing:
The Australian Government has launched its Horizon 2 action plan, the next phase of Australia's national cyber security strategy. This signals continued investment in protecting Australian organisations and is worth watching for any new compliance or reporting requirements that may affect your RTO. Separately, a critical security flaw in Ivanti software was actively exploited by attackers within just 24 hours of it being publicly disclosed — if your organisation uses Ivanti products, check with your IT team immediately to confirm patches have been applied.

There are also fresh warnings that AI tools like Anthropic's Claude can be misused by cybercriminals, reinforcing the need for care when staff use AI assistants with sensitive information. While overall phishing volumes have dropped, attacks are becoming more sophisticated and harder to spot. **Practical tip:** Before clicking any link in an unexpected email, hover over it to preview the destination URL — if it looks unfamiliar or suspicious, report it rather than

featured.title: Government launches Horizon 2 action plan for Australia’s cyber security strategy
featured.quote: Australia's government launches Horizon 2 cyber strategy, directly shaping national security policy.
